law: This sample code project demonstrates how to use ARKit to find devices equipped with Apple Ultra Wideband (UWB) chips using a technique known as ranging .
To use Extended Distance Measurement (EDM) capabilities, you’ll need two iPhone 15 devices or later.

To use the Nearby Interaction framework in your app, it needs to implement the following three processes to use precision device finding: Check for availability of UWB and specific versions according to iOS releases if your app needs capabilities such as EDM.
This sample code project includes one possible implementation of a distance quality estimator function and its use to guide the people using the appropriate UI.
Because there are two types of UWB chips, always check to ensure that the peer devices support the mode the app is interested in, as shown in the example below:

The specific criteria for such estimators are specific to your app’s use case; the following example is one such implementation: Leverage the spatial awareness of ARKit and Apple Ultra Wideband Chips in your app to guide users to a nearby device.
When running on paired devices, both the watch and iPhone must contain an Ultra Wideband chip.
The bundle IDs should be and respectively.
